goal: Reduce public Home DB roundtrips without cache or load-test target shortcuts.
changed: Changed GetHomeQueryHandler to use the Home shell projection.
Implemented HomeQueryStore.GetHomeShellAsync as a single EF projection over home page, singleton site settings, and optional resume asset URL.
Added a Postgres command-count contract test for public Home.
Captured a k6 public Home smoke artifact.
intentionallyNotChanged: No Real Backend target selection change.
No public Work/Blog detail DTO shape change.

validations.name: RED focused Postgres Home command-count test
validations.result: failed before implementation: expected 3 commands, actual 5

validations.name: GREEN focused Postgres Home command-count test
dotnet test backend/WoongBlog.sln --no-restore
validations.result: passed: Component 122/122, Unit 56/56, Architecture 35/35, Integration 223/223, Contract 1 skipped

validations.name: 2CPU/shared-8GiB compose health
validations.result: backend rebuilt and /api/health returned 200 through nginx

validations.name: k6 public Home smoke
validations.result: 1500 requests, 99.99 rps, failed rate 0, p95 25.44 ms, max 581.38 ms

risks: This improves /api/public/home, not the current four public-api-mix URLs directly.
Home still returns ContentJson and may need Page public/admin body split later if it becomes large.
The k6 run is a smoke/regression sanity check, not a 1000 rps capacity proof.
recommendation: Promote through dev CI, then continue with persistent Real Backend diagnostics or measured heavy-detail serialization profiling.
